content:
# IVX FINAL ENTERPRISE QA — EVERY ROOT, CONNECTION, THIRD PARTY, DEPLOYMENT AND LIVE VERIFICATION

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Complete the final end-to-end production audit of IVX, fix every issue found within scope, deploy all approved fixes, and return live evidence.

IMPORTANT:

- Do not return a plan only.
- Do not return narrative claims.
- Do not mark VERIFIED without production evidence.
- Do not claim “0 bugs” unless every discovered critical path passes.
- Do not modify or delete investor, buyer, member, deal, payment, authentication, or financial data without explicit owner approval.
- Do not expose secrets.
- Do not ask for credentials unless a real runtime request proves missing, empty, expired, revoked, 401, or 403.
- Existing unrelated failures must be repaired or precisely documented; do not hide them as “pre-existing.”
- Every test must produce an execution result, exit code, HTTP status, trace ID, or other reproducible proof.

==================================================
CURRENT KNOWN STATE
==================================================

Reported as working:

- Vercel AI Gateway key returns HTTP 200
- AI gateway deployed to Render
- Owner AI provider diagnostics show ready
- Live AI chat responds
- Safe Render environment merge implemented
- Deployment deduplication implemented
- Live Work persistence implemented
- Production health works

Known unresolved or suspicious items:

1. 4 backend tests fail because @supabase/supabase-js cannot resolve in the local backend sandbox.
2. Members count is reported as 4, while prior expected counts included approximately 36.
3. Investors count is reported as 875.
4. Buyers count is reported as 0 or previously 1.
5. Rork-related dependencies and variables may remain in the project.
6. Final physical-device QA and complete third-party verification may still be incomplete.
7. Scale readiness remains separate and must not be represented as 100M verified.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — FREEZE RELEASE BASELINE
==================================================

Before changing anything, record:

- repository
- branch
- local SHA
- remote SHA
- Render deployed SHA
- landing deployed SHA
- mobile app version
- Android build number
- backend service ID
- Render deploy ID
- Supabase project reference
- AI provider
- AI model
- active production API URL
- active landing URL
- active chat URL
- current database counts
- timestamp and timezone

Create one final QA run ID and one root trace ID.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — DISCOVER EVERY PRODUCTION ROOT
==================================================

Inspect the repository and runtime to discover every active route.

Audit at minimum:

PUBLIC:

- /
- landing
- registration
- waitlist
- login
- password recovery
- deal list
- deal detail
- reels
- media
- search
- public chat, if enabled
- health
- readiness
- version

OWNER:

- owner login
- owner dashboard
- Owner AI
- developer workspace
- Live Work
- variables
- diagnostics
- members
- investors
- buyers
- CRM
- properties
- deals
- revenue
- transactions
- analytics
- autonomous dashboard
- notifications
- settings
- deployment controls

MOBILE:

- Home
- Invest
- Market
- Portfolio
- Chat
- Profile
- CRM
- Reels
- Owner Login
- Deal Detail
- View Deal
- Invest Now
- notifications
- uploads
- deep links

BACKEND:

- auth
- owner auth
- member APIs
- investor APIs
- buyer APIs
- CRM APIs
- property APIs
- deal APIs
- reel APIs
- chat APIs
- Owner AI APIs
- developer runtime APIs
- attachment APIs
- notification APIs
- autonomous APIs
- deployment APIs
- diagnostic APIs

For every route return:

- route
- method
- source file
- auth requirement
- expected status
- actual status
- latency
- response schema
- trace ID
- PASS/FAIL

==================================================
PHASE 3 — THIRD-PARTY CONNECTION AUDIT
==================================================

Verify every production integration independently.

A. GITHUB

Test:

- authenticated identity
- repository read
- branch read
- latest SHA
- file read
- controlled commit
- push
- remote SHA confirmation

B. RENDER

Test:

- service read
- environment read
- safe environment merge
- deployment trigger
- duplicate deployment prevention
- deployment status
- deployed SHA
- health
- readiness
- rollback reference

C. SUPABASE

Test:

- Auth
- database
- REST
- Realtime
- Storage
- RLS
- service-role server access
- anon client access
- connection pooling
- migration status
- index status
- backup availability

D. AI PROVIDER

Test:

- provider
- model
- API endpoint family
- adapter version
- credential validity
- text request
- long request
- image request
- PDF request
- timeout
- fallback
- trace ID

E. EXPO / EAS

Test:

- project binding
- app config
- production API URL
- build profile
- Android build
- iOS configuration
- update channel
- signing configuration

F. NOTIFICATIONS

Test:

- Expo push
- FCM
- APNs configuration
- owner notification
- in-app notification
- duplicate prevention
- deep link

G. STORAGE / CDN

Test:

- image upload
- PDF upload
- video upload initialization
- signed URL
- read URL
- expiration
- CDN
- cache
- media fallback

H. REDIS

Test:

- deployment status
- connection
- cache
- distributed rate limiting
- Socket.IO adapter
- failure behavior

I. DNS / TLS

Test:

- ivxholding.com
- chat.ivxholding.com
- api.ivxholding.com
- certificate validity
- redirects
- DNS target
- HTTPS
- CORS

For each integration return:

- service
- environment
- credential variable
- loaded true/false
- safe authentication result
- HTTP/result status
- latency
- trace ID
- PASS/FAIL

Never display secret values.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — FIX THE BACKEND TEST ENVIRONMENT
==================================================

The report currently states:

4 tests fail because @supabase/supabase-js cannot resolve in the local backend sandbox.

This must not remain unresolved.

Inspect:

- root package.json
- backend package.json
- workspaces
- lockfile
- module resolution
- Bun configuration
- tsconfig paths
- test runner configuration
- production dependency installation

Fix the dependency structure so the backend tests execute from a reproducible clean install.

Required outcome:

- @supabase/supabase-js declared in the correct package
- no dependence on accidental root-only resolution
- clean install works
- backend tests resolve modules
- all 260 tests execute
- no missing-module failures

Run:

- clean dependency install
- dependency tree inspection
- backend test suite
- full test suite
- typecheck
- production build

Do not accept 256/260 as 100%.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — DATA RECONCILIATION
==================================================

Current reported counts require forensic verification:

- Members: 4
- Investors: 875
- Buyers: 0

Prior expected values include:

- approximately 36 members
- approximately 850 investors and buyers

Do not delete, merge, recreate, or restore anything without owner approval.

Audit every relevant Supabase table, view, schema, RLS policy, API query, filter, role mapping, pagination rule, and frontend count.

Reconcile:

DATABASE PHYSICAL COUNTS

- members
- profiles
- auth.users
- investors
- buyers
- leads
- prospects
- waitlist
- landing registrations
- archived
- soft-deleted
- legacy records

API COUNTS

- members
- investors
- buyers
- CRM summary

UI COUNTS

- Members screen
- Investors screen
- Buyers screen
- CRM dashboard

Return exact reason for every variance.

No final verification while database, API, and UI counts disagree without a documented explanation.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — AUTHENTICATION AND AUTHORIZATION
==================================================

Test:

1. Owner login
2. Owner logout
3. Owner login again
4. Session persistence
5. Expired session
6. Session refresh
7. Invalid password
8. Password recovery
9. Missing token
10. Invalid token
11. Non-owner access
12. Admin access
13. Member access
14. Investor access
15. Buyer access
16. Owner-only developer tools
17. Owner-only variables
18. Owner-only diagnostics
19. Owner-only CRM data
20. Destructive-operation approval

Return HTTP status, route, role, expected result, actual result, and trace ID.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — OWNER AI AND SENIOR-DEVELOPER BRAIN
==================================================

Test normal Owner AI:

- short message
- long message
- conversation context
- persistence
- reload
- image
- PDF
- timeout
- provider failure
- no duplicate response

Test senior-developer execution from the actual production IVX chat:

1. Authenticate owner.
2. Submit a development task.
3. Confirm correct intent classification.
4. Confirm worker selected.
5. Confirm tools loaded.
6. Confirm variables validated.
7. Confirm repository inspection.
8. Confirm real command execution.
9. Confirm code diff.
10. Confirm tests.
11. Confirm commit.
12. Confirm push.
13. Confirm deployment.
14. Confirm production verification.
15. Confirm structured evidence response.

Live Work must display persisted real events:

RECEIVED
AUTHORIZED
VARIABLES_VALIDATING
INSPECTING
PATCHING
TESTING
COMMITTING
PUSHING
DEPLOYING
VERIFYING
VERIFIED

Test failure states:

FAILED
BLOCKED
CANCELLED
TIMED_OUT

Do not allow narrative-only fallback for an authorized developer task.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — MOBILE APP QA
==================================================

Run on a real Android device and, where available, an iOS test environment.

Test:

- clean install
- cold start
- warm start
- background/resume
- app restart
- network disconnect/reconnect
- slow network
- Android back button
- deep links
- status bar
- safe areas
- bottom navigation
- keyboard
- orientation
- small screen
- large screen
- tablet

Modules:

- Home
- Invest
- Market
- Portfolio
- Chat
- Profile
- CRM
- Reels
- Owner Login
- Owner AI

Verify Home design:

- first 3 regular investment cards
- up to 8 pictures per card
- horizontal image carousel
- Casa Rosario reel after the first 3 cards
- no conversion of every card into a reel

Verify Reels design:

- one reel fits usable viewport
- no clipping
- no overlap
- no stretched media
- no bottom-tab collision
- no infinite loading
- View Deal works
- Invest Now works

==================================================
PHASE 9 — LANDING PAGE QA
==================================================

Test:

- mobile
- tablet
- desktop
- registration
- login
- deal cards
- mixed card/reel feed
- media
- search
- View Deal
- Invest Now
- no jumping
- no layout shift
- no stale data
- no mock data
- correct production API
- correct registration-to-Supabase flow

==================================================
PHASE 10 — CHAT AND ATTACHMENTS
==================================================

Test:

- send
- receive
- reply
- copy
- pin
- message persistence
- restart persistence
- ordering
- duplicate prevention
- scroll-to-latest
- keyboard behavior
- image upload
- PDF upload
- video metadata
- signed URLs
- failure and retry
- owner chat
- normal user chat
- realtime reconnect

Acceptance:

- zero missing messages
- zero duplicate message IDs
- deterministic ordering
- persisted after backend restart

==================================================
PHASE 11 — DEALS AND INVESTMENT FLOW
==================================================

Test every entry point:

- Home → Deal
- Reels → Deal
- Landing → Deal
- Search → Deal
- Saved → Deal
- Portfolio → Deal
- Notification → Deal
- shared deep link → Deal
- direct URL → Deal

Test:

- Casa Rosario
- Jacksonville
- One Stop Development
- invalid ID
- deleted ID
- API timeout
- 401
- 404
- 500

Verify:

- correct ID mapping
- no infinite Loading Deal
- View Deal works
- Invest Now works
- back navigation restores previous position
- no duplicate investment action

Do not execute real investment or payment transactions during QA.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — AUTONOMOUS
==================================================

Verify whether Autonomous is:

- plan only
- code present
- deployed
- enabled
- running
- producing records
- sending notifications
- generating daily reports

Test:

- worker heartbeat
- scheduler
- queue
- live dashboard
- prospect creation
- deduplication
- qualification
- purchasing-intent notification
- daily report
- pause
- resume
- retry
- restart recovery

Return actual job IDs and trace IDs.

==================================================
PHASE 13 — SECURITY
==================================================

Audit:

- secrets in source
- secrets in Expo public variables
- secrets in logs
- Rork variables
- Rork runtime network calls
- owner route exposure
- RLS
- CORS
- rate limiting
- brute-force protection
- upload validation
- malformed input
- oversized payload
- SQL injection
- XSS
- CSRF where relevant
- log injection
- dependency vulnerabilities
- security headers

Required:

- no secret in EXPO_PUBLIC variables
- no unauthorized owner access
- no service-role key in client
- no runtime Rork dependency unless explicitly approved

==================================================
PHASE 14 — PERFORMANCE AND OBSERVABILITY
==================================================

Verify:

- Redis active or accurately reported inactive
- autoscaling configuration
- connection pooling
- database indexes applied
- cursor pagination
- compression
- external APM
- metrics endpoint
- alerting
- log correlation
- trace IDs

Run safe staged load tests:

- 10 concurrent
- 50 concurrent
- 100 concurrent
- 500 concurrent

Return:

- p50
- p95
- p99
- RPS
- error rate
- CPU
- memory
- database connections
- breaking point

Do not claim 100M readiness.

==================================================
PHASE 15 — REGRESSION AND BUILD
==================================================

Run from a clean environment:

- dependency install
- lint
- typecheck
- unit tests
- integration tests
- backend full suite
- frontend tests
- route tests
- RLS tests
- Owner AI tests
- developer runtime tests
- mobile build validation
- production web build
- Android signed build

Required outcome:

- all in-scope tests pass
- no missing-module failures
- no hidden P0/P1 failures
- unrelated failures listed and resolved or formally excluded with owner approval

==================================================
PHASE 16 — DEPLOYMENT
==================================================

After fixes:

1. Review final diff.
2. Commit approved changes.
3. Push to owner-controlled GitHub.
4. Confirm remote SHA.
5. Deploy backend.
6. Deploy landing.
7. Build Android APK.
8. Deploy mobile update if applicable.
9. Confirm production SHA matches GitHub.
10. Verify health.
11. Verify readiness.
12. Verify all critical live routes.
13. Capture rollback SHA.

Prevent duplicate deployment for the same SHA.

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL REPORT
==================================================

[exact final QA scope]

- Root trace ID:
- Start:
- Finish:

DATA RECONCILIATION:

- Investors database/API/UI:
- Buyers database/API/UI:
- Variance explanation:
- Data loss confirmed: YES/NO

AUTH:

DEPLOYMENT PROOF:

- Landing deploy ID:
- Android version/build:

- Owner AI response ID:

FINAL VERIFICATION RULE:

Use VERIFIED only when:

- every active production root has been discovered and tested
- every required third-party integration passes
- all backend tests pass, including the 4 Supabase module failures
- data counts are reconciled across database, API, and UI
- Owner AI works
- senior-developer execution works from the actual IVX app
- GitHub commit and remote SHA match
- Render deployment ID exists
- deployed SHA matches GitHub
- mobile real-device QA passes
- landing QA passes
- no P0/P1 issue remains
- rollback is documented
- no secret is exposed
- no unauthorized business-data change occurs

Do not state “0 gaps” or “0 bugs” unless the final report contains zero failed critical tests and reproducible live evidence.
